meta.title: MergeCore Filament Rules
meta.description: Production-focused rules for Filament v3 resources, tables, forms, actions, policies, and multi-tenant panels. Use with project metadata indicating Filament is installed.
meta.notes: Heuristic detection: confirm against your Filament version, panel provider, and tenancy package. Rules marked filament: true may be gated by the host when the stack is present.

scoring.normalisation: For each review unit (typically one PHP file or one git diff hunk set), sum applicable rule penalties, apply quality_signal multiplier to each rule where host supports it, then: score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))). Cap prevents a single file from driving the whole review to zero unless the host uses multi-file aggregation.

filament_overrides.description: Optional host tuning: multiply penalties for rules in category 'tables' when the file is *Table.php or contains table() by >400 lines—reflects user-visible latency impact.

interpretation.9_to_10: Panel-safe: queries scoped, tables eager-loaded, forms bounded, policies aligned with navigation.
interpretation.7_to_8: Shippable with fixes: occasional N+1 or heavy form; address before high-traffic pages.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: visible latency, authorisation drift, or tenancy ambiguity.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: tenancy leak, missing policy gates, or unbounded data exposure in admin.

rules.title: Bloated resource class
rules.description: A single Resource carrying huge table(), form(), infolist(), and many custom pages becomes hard to review and merge. Split concerns into dedicated classes, traits, or sub-resources.

heuristics: Single class file exceeds ~400–500 lines
Multiple unrelated concerns (table + form + many Page::make) in one file
More than ~8 relation managers without extraction

examples.before: class OrderResource extends Resource { public static function table(Table $table): Table { /* 200 lines */ } public static function form(Form $form): Form { /* 200 lines */ } /* + 5 inline Page classes */ }
examples.after: Extract OrderTable, OrderForm, or dedicated ListOrders page classes; keep Resource as wiring only.

rules.title: Navigation and model policy drift
rules.description: Resource shouldRegisterNavigation, canViewAny, and model policies should agree; hidden resources still reachable via direct URL if policies are wrong.

heuristics: shouldRegisterNavigation false but no policy denial on viewAny

examples.before: public static function shouldRegisterNavigation(): bool { return false; } // but Policy::viewAny returns true for all admins
examples.after: Align Policy::viewAny with who may list; use cluster grouping instead of hiding nav while leaving routes open.

rules.title: Slow table: per-row queries in columns
rules.description: TextColumn::formatStateUsing, badge(), or custom components that query the database per record cause N+1 and admin timeouts.

heuristics: Closure body contains ::find(
first()
count() inside column definition

examples.after: withCount('items') on getEloquentQuery() or selectSub for aggregates; eager load what columns need.

rules.title: Slow table: missing eager loads for displayed relations
rules.description: Listing related labels without with() on the table query forces lazy loads for every row.

heuristics: Column uses dot notation or relationship() without eager load in getEloquentQuery/modifyQueryUsing

rules.title: Table without sensible default pagination or sort
rules.description: Large datasets without default sort on an indexed column and without pagination defaults stress the database and UX.

heuristics: Big model behind resource
No ->defaultSort(
No ->paginated([...]) or default page size tuned for data volume

examples.before: public static function table(Table $table): Table { return $table->columns([...]); } // millions of rows possible

rules.title: Tenancy leak: broadened Eloquent query
rules.description: Overriding getEloquentQuery to remove global scopes or omit tenant predicates exposes other organisations’ rows in the panel.

heuristics: Parent getEloquentQuery not called
Tenant filter only from unvalidated request input

examples.before: public static function getEloquentQuery(): Builder { return parent::getEloquentQuery()->withoutGlobalScopes(); }
examples.after: Keep tenant scopes; if you must relax, constrain with an explicit where('tenant_id', Filament::getTenant()?->id) from trusted context.

rules.title: Tenancy leak: tenant id from raw input
rules.description: Using request()->input('tenant_id') or similar inside Filament queries lets clients pivot across tenants unless the value is cryptographically bound to the session.

examples.after: Resolve current team from Filament tenant, authenticated membership, or route binding validated against the user.

rules.title: Poor action: heavy side effects in handle
rules.description: Filament table/header actions that perform synchronous HTTP, long loops, or mail sends block the Livewire request and feel broken to admins.

heuristics: action closure > 40 lines
nested DB without transaction where money involved

rules.title: Poor action: missing authorisation
rules.description: Destructive or sensitive actions must call policies or $user->can() explicitly; Filament does not guess your domain permissions for custom actions.

heuristics: Action mutates models or calls APIs
No authorize(), Gate::, or $this->authorize in same block

examples.after: Use built-in delete with policy, or ->authorize('delete', $record) before mutate.

rules.title: Duplicated action logic across resources
rules.description: Copy-pasted bulk actions diverge; centralise in invokable classes, traits, or domain services.

heuristics: Same BulkAction string label in multiple files
Identical closure bodies across OrderResource and InvoiceResource

examples.before: Two resources each define 'Recalculate totals' with 30-line closures

rules.title: Form schema abuse: fat closures and side effects
rules.description: Huge form() methods and afterStateUpdated hooks that hit APIs or mutate unrelated models are hard to test and cause surprise writes.

heuristics: afterStateUpdated contains Http::, Mail::, or multiple DB writes
form() exceeds ~200 lines without extraction

examples.after: Dispatch job on save, or debounced custom Livewire step with clear UX feedback.

rules.title: Form schema abuse: excessive live() without debounce
rules.description: Marking many fields live() triggers round-trips on every keystroke; combine fields, use debounce, or validate on submit.

heuristics: More than ~6 live fields in one form
No ->debounce(

examples.before: Ten TextInputs each ->live() for a simple create form
examples.after: live(onBlur: true) or a single Section::make()->live() with debounce(500).

rules.title: Repeater / relationship builder loading unbounded children
rules.description: Relationship repeaters without limits or pagination can load thousands of related models into the form.

heuristics: No ->maxItems(
No query scoping on relationship options

examples.before: Repeater::make('lineItems')->relationship() on orders with hundreds of lines
examples.after: maxItems, lazy loading via separate relation manager, or dedicated edit page per child.

rules.title: Policy mistake: missing Filament model policy methods
rules.description: Filament checks viewAny, create, update, delete on the model policy. Missing methods default to deny or allow depending on Laravel version and gate setup—explicit methods prevent accidents.

heuristics: Policy exists for model used in Resource
Resource registered but Policy lacks viewAny/view/create/update/delete

examples.before: class OrderPolicy { public function delete(User $u, Order $o): bool { ... } // no viewAny
examples.after: Add viewAny, view, create, update mirroring your roles and tenant membership.

rules.title: Policy mistake: Resource can* out of sync with Policy
rules.description: Overriding canCreate, canEdit, canDelete on the Resource while Policy returns different answers confuses maintainers and can leave holes for direct route access.

heuristics: Resource overrides can* without delegating to Gate or parent policy

examples.before: public static function canCreate(): bool { return auth()->user()->isAdmin; } // Policy::create says editors OK

rules.title: Policy mistake: viewAny too permissive for sensitive resources
rules.description: Listing resources exposes metadata; viewAny true for every authenticated user is often wrong for billing, HR, or tenant data.

heuristics: viewAny returns true unconditionally
Sensitive model names in Resource (Invoice, Salary, ApiToken)

examples.before: public function viewAny(User $user): bool { return true; }
examples.after: Gate on role, permission, or tenant role; align with navigation clusters.

rules.title: Strong table query: explicit with() and constrained counts
rules.description: modifyQueryUsing or getEloquentQuery with with() and withCount() shows deliberate performance awareness.
